title: qPCR for Differentiation of Bovine, Porcine, and Poultry Components
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Accurate     differentiation     of     bovine  ,   porcine  ,   and     poultry     components   in   food     products     is     essential     for     regulatory     compliance  ,   consumer     trust  ,   and     religious     dietary     requirements  .   Quantitative     Polymerase     Chain     Reaction   (  qPCR  )   offers     a     sensitive     and     specific     approach     to     detect     and     quantify     animal   DNA in   raw     and     processed     food     matrices  .   This     technique     provides     rapid     results     within     hours  ,   compared     to     conventional     methods     that     may     take     days  .   Its     ability     to     work     with     highly     processed     samples     makes     it   ideal   for   modern   supply     chains     where     ingredient     integrity     is     critical  .   Implementing     qPCR   testing as   a     routine     part     of     food     surveillance     will     reduce     cases     of     mislabeling  ,   prevent     food     fraud  ,   and     ensure     compliance     with   halal,   kosher  ,   and     international     labeling     standards  ,   thereby     strengthening     food     safety     systems  .
